heroPoems.title: Beyond the Three Qualities
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Nature moves through three strands:
clarity,
passion,
and inertia.
These qualities bind the deathless self
to body and behavior.

heroPoems.title: On Children
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: No luck on my begging rounds today
heroPoems.firstStanza:
No luck on my begging rounds today.
From village to village
I dragged this body.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: Wild nights! Wild nights!
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Wild nights! Wild nights!
Were I with thee,
Wild nights should be
Our luxury!

heroPoems.title: Dust Returning
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Dust returns to the earth
as it was.
Breath returns to God
who gave it.

heroPoems.title: Where the mind is without fear and the
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: The Need of Being Versed in Country Things
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The house had gone to bring again
To the midnight sky a sunset glow.
Now the chimney was all of the house that stood,
Like a pistil after the petals go.
The barn opposed across the way,
That would have joined the house in flame
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: I Live My Life in Widening Circles
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I live my life in widening circles
that stretch over all things.
I may not complete this final one,
but I give myself to it.
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: I Am!
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I am! yet what I am none cares or knows,
My friends forsake me like a memory lost;
I am the self-consumer of my woes—
They rise and vanish in oblivious host,
Like shadows in love's frenzied, stifled throes,
And yet I am, and live—like vapours tost.

heroPoems.title: Valley Spirit
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The valley spirit never dies.
This is the dark, fertile source.

collections.name: The Folded Map
collections.tagline: For when rain writes on windows.
collections.theme: An atmosphere of low light, small rituals, and unhurried noticing.

featuredPoem.title: History Needs Exemplars
featuredPoem.firstLines:
If the best stop showing up,
standards collapse

poems.title: Bards of Passion and of Mirth
The rose is nothing without the beloved's face

collections.name: A House of Soft Echoes
collections.tagline: For when pages feel like shelter.
collections.theme: A compact emotional landscape of return, pause, and half-spoken thought.

featuredPoem.title: Sonnets to Orpheus II, 10
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Everything calls us to celebrate.
No season refuses us.

poems.title: The brain is wider than the sky
Remember Me at the Last Breath

collections.name: What the Silence Kept
collections.tagline: For when the hallway feels endless.
collections.theme: A shelf of poems for evenings when ordinary objects feel quietly charged with meaning.

featuredPoem.title: Do Not Awaken Love
featuredPoem.firstLines:
I charge you, daughters of Jerusalem,
by gazelles and does of the field:

poems.title: The nights of separation are over at last
